Gender Equality.reason: The course dedicates a week to "Feminism in IR," directly analyzing gender perspectives in international politics, which relates to promoting gender equality in policy (Target 5.c).
evidence: Sandra Whitworth, “Feminism” & Jacqui True, “The Ethics of Feminism”

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly analyzes power imbalances through topics like "Asymmetric Structure and the Small States in IR" and "Non-Western IR," directly addressing inequality between nations (Target 10.6).

the unbalanced phenomena of the IR research

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about theories of "international security" and "international cooperation," directly addressing the core themes of peace, justice, and global institutions (Targets 16.1, 16.3, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course analyzes theories of "international cooperation" and "Neo-liberal Institutionalism," providing the theoretical basis for understanding global partnerships and governance (Targets 17.14, 17.16).
